matched.reason: Both describe Errtu's relentless pursuit of the Crystal Shard Crenshinibon.
Both indicate the crystal shard will be found and claimed by a wielder.
Both refer to the Hosttower wizards' betrayal of Akar Kessell.
Both describe Crenshinibon's manipulation and control of its wielder Kessell.
Both detail Kessell's rise as a tyrant conquering Ten-Towns with the shard's power.
Both concern Regis's past with Pasha Pook's thieves' guild catching up to him.
Both describe a massive united barbarian army preparing to attack Ten-Towns.
Both express Bruenor's deep desire to reclaim the lost dwarven homeland Mithril Hall.
Both specify Bruenor forging the magical warhammer Aegis-fang for Wulfgar.
Both refer to Wulfgar's secret personal vow/quest, which involves slaying the dragon Icingdeath.
Both state Drizzt will train Wulfgar in combat.
Both describe Drizzt acquiring a magical scimitar from the dragon's hoard.
Both involve the companions setting out on a quest to find Mithril Hall.
Both announce the assassin Artemis Entreri hunting Regis in Ten-Towns.

unmatchedGold.promise_text: Regis's ruby pendant is a stolen magical charm that can influence others.
Drizzt will face mistrust and prejudice from the people of Ten-Towns because he is a dark elf.
Drizzt has a magical panther companion, Guenhwyvar, summoned from an onyx figurine.
The southerner deBernezan is betraying Ten-Towns by aiding the barbarian invasion.
The young barbarian standard bearer is a significant figure with potential for greatness.
The crystal tower Cryshal-Tirith will be Kessell's base of power.
Drizzt and Bruenor plan to use Regis and his ruby pendant to sway the council of Ten-Towns to unite against the barbarians.
Heafstaag will survive the Battle of Bryn Shander and return as an antagonist.
Wulfgar, a barbarian indentured to Bruenor, has a vow to repay him and a complicated relationship.
Kessell uses magical mirrors to scry and communicate with his forces.
Wulfgar vows to kill the dragon Icingdeath and claim its treasure.
Wulfgar intends to challenge Heafstaag for kingship of the tribe.
Kessell's tower has the power to destroy a city with a beam of light, and he will use it against Bryn Shander if they don't surrender.
Regis will act as emissary to Kessell in an attempt to delay the attack on Bryn Shander.
Regis will trick Kessell into believing there are traitor orcs in his army, causing him to attack his own forces.
Drizzt and Guenhwyvar will infiltrate Cryshal-Tirith to confront Kessell and end his threat.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Some wizards in the caravan have darker, hidden purposes.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: The caravan's destination is Ten-Towns under Kelvin's Cairn, sought for its scrimshaw trade — though some wizards have darker purposes.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "How fortunate to find so convenient a tool," Dendybar noted.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Regis's ruby pendant possesses magical persuasive powers.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: But it is there - I see it clearly and know the legend of such a stone.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Ten-seconds passed and Kemp had not blinked.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: The warhammer Aegis-fang will be given to Wulfgar.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt teases Bruenor about gifting the warhammer to Wulfgar while genuinely admiring its craftsmanship.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "I'll place no bonds on yer will, but I'll have yer oath, for me own good conscience, that ye'll never raise this weapon against the people o' Ten-Tbwns!"

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Errtu will answer the summoning and come to the material plane to seek Crenshinibon.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: On the Abyss, Errtu hears the weak summoning call and chooses to answer not out of compulsion but because it has been waiting for years for a path to the material plane.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Now the grotesque, monkeylike head, with the maw and muzzle of a dog and the oversized incisors of a boar, was visible, the huge, blood-red eyes squinting from within the brazier's flame.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Errtu will eventually betray Kessell and try to take the shard.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Errtu privately judges Kessell unfit to lead and reveals it secretly offered the shard a deal to replace him, which Crenshinibon refused because it prefers a weak master.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "The tower's walls reflect any attacks, but the crystal heart is its only vulnerability."

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Wulfgar will challenge and confront Heafstaag for leadership of the barbarian tribe.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Wulfgar is shamed to learn his own tribe follows the one-eyed Heafstaag; Drizzt orders a warning sent to Bryn Shander.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "I am Wulfgar, son of Beornegar... I have come to challenge Heafstaag for leadership of the Tribe of the Elk!"

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Drizzt will summon Errtu using his true name to confront him.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt recognizes not just the demon type but the specific creature—Errtu, a demon he knew from Menzoberranzan—and realizes he knows its true name and can summon it.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: And so Errtu was quite receptive, even relieved, when it heard Drizzt's call floating down the valley.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Errtu vows a century-long revenge after being banished by Drizzt.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Errtu is banished, issuing its century-long vow of revenge as it dissolves into steam and vapor while the scimitar clangs to the stone.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "I shall return for you, Drizzt Do'Urden! A century shall pass before you see the last of Errtu!"
